Monday: Margaret opened with "Jalapeño corn dog bites. We're doing it this week and I need to know right..." - questioning whether the dish had real technique behind it. Tuesday: Margaret confirmed a 60-40 cornmeal-to-flour minimum after testing - "Marcus gets it. Fine. Cornmeal-to-flour ratio is 60-40 minimum or the seal breaks down in the oil." Marcus framed this as a performance threshold, not a limitation. Stephanie agreed the copy should anchor on crust performance rather than ingredients. All three aligned on shipping.

Wednesday: Julian presented three bite-through shots. Stephanie pushed for shot two - the crisp exterior shattering - saying "Shot two's the one - if people are stopping their scroll, it's because they see that crisp exterior breaking apart, right?" Margaret agreed, prioritizing the crisp over the jalapeno cross-section Julian had championed. The team locked in shot two.

Thursday: Marcus brought finalized copy focusing on cornmeal's moisture-seal function. Margaret pushed to lead with the steam-absorption mechanic - not taste. Stephanie flagged repetition: "Love that you went all in on the moisture-seal thing - let's see what you've got?" Marcus tightened the structure to lead with cornmeal arresting moisture, then pivot to texture. Margaret approved - cut the explanation, show the fry result.

Friday: Margaret opened with "Final review. Shot two locked, copy locked. Someone tell me what isn't right, because something..." - but the team had no notes. Marcus credited trusting the work over overthinking it. Stephanie noted the team listened instead of defending positions. Julian and Marcus agreed restraint drove both the shot and copy success.

Saturday: This conversation contains no discussion of "Jalapeno Corn Dog Bites" - it is entirely about a website deployment. Devon confirmed verification passed and pushed the site live. Margaret commented that restraint leads to cleaner results, which Devon agreed with. No food, ingredients, or recipes were discussed.
